scenario: Beacon, a fintech subscriber to Camunda 8.8 SaaS processing 5400 transactions per day, scaffolds a new Node.js TypeScript project for the Orchestration Cluster API. A senior engineer must follow the documented Get started steps so that the project has TypeScript configured and the SDK installed as a runtime dependency before the team writes the first worker module.
question: Which npm command does the Get started guide list as the step that installs the SDK as a runtime dependency?

options.text: Run npm i @camunda8/sdk in the project directory after npm init and npm install -D typescript, which adds the SDK as a runtime dependency in package.json
Run npm i -D @camunda8/sdk in the project directory after npm init and npm install -D typescript, which adds the SDK as a development-only dependency in package.json
Run npm i camunda-platform-8 in the project directory after npm init and npm install -D typescript, which adds the legacy meta-package as a runtime dependency in package.json
Run npm i zeebe-node in the project directory after npm init and npm install -D typescript, which adds the historical Zeebe-only package as a runtime dependency in package.json

a.text: Correct. The Get started steps say to install the SDK as a dependency with npm i @camunda8/sdk, after creating the project with npm init -y and adding TypeScript via npm install -D typescript.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs install @camunda8/sdk as a regular runtime dependency with npm i, not as a dev dependency; only the typescript tooling itself is installed with -D.

c.text: Incorrect. The published quickstart package is @camunda8/sdk; camunda-platform-8 is not the package listed in the Get started guide.

d.text: Incorrect. The Get started guide installs @camunda8/sdk, the unified Camunda 8 SDK for Node.js; zeebe-node is not the package referenced in the quickstart.

explanation: The Get started guide creates a Node.js TypeScript project with npm init -y, adds TypeScript with npm install -D typescript and npx tsc --init, then installs the SDK as a runtime dependency with npm i @camunda8/sdk.
